sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Napoli have shown inconsistency in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins and 3 losses, including a heavy 6-2 defeat to PSV Eindhoven. Inter, on the other hand, are in strong form, remaining unbeaten with 4 wins and 1 draw, scoring 13 goals in their last 5 outings. This suggests Inter hold a clear edge going into the match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: The last two encounters between Napoli and Inter have both ended in 1-1 draws, indicating tightly contested battles. Over the last 5 head-to-heads, Inter have not lost to Napoli, with 2 wins and 3 draws, showing a slight historical dominance.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Napoli dominate in possession (63%, league rank 1) and create numerous chances (7.14 corners per game, rank 2), but struggle to convert big opportunities (41% conversion rate). Inter have been clinical, scoring 18 goals this season (rank 2), with key players like Lautaro Martínez in top form (5 goals in last 5 matches). Napoli's defensive vulnerabilities (no clean sheet in 8 games) could be exploited by Inter's attack.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Napoli, maintaining control in midfield will be crucial, with players like Stanislav Lobotka (7.7 rating in last H2H) key to their game plan. Inter’s attacking threat lies with Lautaro Martínez and support from Federico Dimarco (1 goal, 2 assists recently), making them dangerous on the counter.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Inter's superior form and Napoli's defensive struggles, combined with Sofascore’s close prediction (Napoli 40%, Draw 22%, Inter 38%), a narrow victory for Inter seems likely. Expect a competitive game with both teams scoring, but Inter’s attacking edge could decide the outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Stadio Diego Armando Maradona saw Napoli take a narrow lead over Inter with a penalty converted by Kevin De Bruyne in the 33rd minute. Despite Inter dominating possession and creating more big chances, they failed to capitalize, missing key opportunities and hitting the woodwork twice. Napoli, on the other hand, made the most of their limited chances, securing the advantage going into the break. Both teams showed competitive intent, with close stats in final third entries and penalty area presence, indicating a tightly contested match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game began with Inter asserting early dominance, maintaining consistent attacking pressure for the opening minutes. However, Napoli gradually found their footing with a noticeable shift in momentum around the middle of the half, culminating in a strong spell of pressure that led to their goal. Towards the end of the half, Inter regained their attacking rhythm, pushing hard for an equalizer with a series of threatening moves, but they couldn't break through Napoli's defense before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Napoli, Kevin De Bruyne stood out with his clinical penalty finish, proving decisive in giving his team the lead. Giovanni Di Lorenzo also impressed with a strong defensive display and contributed to maintaining structure at the back. On Inter's side, Lautaro Martínez was a constant threat despite missing big chances, showing intent to get on the scoresheet. Hakan Çalhanoğlu played a pivotal role in midfield, creating opportunities and maintaining composure under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Napoli adopted a more pragmatic approach, focusing on defending their lead after scoring while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Their defensive organization, led by players like Juan Jesus, was effective in neutralizing Inter's attacks. Inter, meanwhile, controlled possession for large parts of the half and pushed forward aggressively, but their inability to convert chances highlighted a need for sharper finishing. Their high press in the final third showed promise but left them vulnerable to quick transitions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Inter come out with increased intensity as they chase an equalizer. They have the attacking tools to turn the game around if they can improve their finishing. Napoli will probably aim to protect their lead, focusing on a compact defensive shape while seeking to exploit spaces on the counter. The match could open up if Inter commit more players forward, potentially leading to goals for either side. Discipline will be key for Napoli, as they’ve already picked up a couple of yellow cards.
